finding: get_packages() iterates ALL installed distributions on every import pubrun (full-environment mode). Large venvs add 50-200ms.
evidence: capture/packages.py:37-55 — importlib.metadata.distributions() fully iterated with locate_file and read_text per dist.
proposed_action: Change default to imported-only mode; full-environment becomes opt-in.

finding: get_hardware() spawns external subprocesses (nvidia-smi/sysctl/system_profiler/wmic) synchronously during import. GPU detection alone can take 200-500ms.
evidence: capture/hardware.py:78-145 — subprocess.check_output calls with no caching.
proposed_action: Defer hardware detection to a background thread; collect results at stop() time.

finding: get_git() spawns 4 sequential git subprocess calls each with 1s timeout. On network-mounted or large repos git status --porcelain can be slow.
proposed_action: Parallelize git calls or make dirty-check configurable.

finding: get_invocation() hashes entire script file with SHA-256 on every import. Large scripts or notebooks block startup.
evidence: capture/invocation.py:67-73 — hashlib.sha256() over full file read.
proposed_action: Gate hashing behind size threshold (skip for files >= 1MB).

finding: TqdmSafeTee.write() calls datetime.now().strftime() on every line in timestamped mode. Per-line overhead in tight print loops.
evidence: capture/console.py:57-60 — inside inner loop over lines.
proposed_action: Format timestamp once per write() call rather than per line.

finding: EventStream.emit() does json.dumps + write + flush under lock for every event. Lock + flush is a serialization bottleneck for high-frequency resource samples.
evidence: events.py:62-77 — self._lock held during full serialize+write+flush.
proposed_action: Buffer events; flush every 100 events or 1s. Move json.dumps outside lock.

finding: _register_provenance() re-reads the ENTIRE file to compute SHA-256 on close even though data was already hashed incrementally during read. Redundant full I/O.
evidence: core.py:354-362 — opens file rb and hashes 64KB chunks duplicating self._hash.
proposed_action: Use self._hash.hexdigest() for read-mode; only re-hash for write-mode.

finding: Every read/readline/__next__ call does isinstance check + conditional .encode() even for binary-mode files where data is already bytes.
evidence: core.py:297-302 — isinstance(data bytes) check on every I/O call.
proposed_action: Detect mode at construction; skip branch for known mode. Full class split deferred.

finding: scan_runs() instantiates RunInfo for every directory doing synchronous JSON I/O for each. O(n) sequential parses with no caching or pagination.
evidence: status.py:284-296 — for entry in base.iterdir(): runs.append(RunInfo(entry)).
proposed_action: Add lightweight index file (.pubrun-index.json) for cached metadata.

finding: RunInfo._load_from_lock() reads entire events.jsonl (potentially MB) just to count lines for status display.
evidence: status.py:223-227 — sum(1 for _ in f) on potentially large file.
proposed_action: Use file size estimate or maintain count in sidecar/lock file.

finding: resolve_config() reads and deep-merges default.toml from package resources on every Run() creation. default.toml never changes at runtime.
evidence: config.py:95-131 — load_default_config() called fresh each time.
proposed_action: Cache load_default_config() result at module level.

finding: redact_argv() recompiles the secret regex via re.compile() on every subprocess spawn inside the patched Popen.__init__ (under lock).
proposed_action: Cache compiled regex at module level.

finding: redact_env_vars() calls is_secret_key() per env var which calls _get_secret_pattern() recompiling regex each time — O(n) compilations.
proposed_action: Same fix as PERF-12: cache the compiled pattern.

finding: On Windows/macOS _poll_rss() spawns a subprocess (wmic/ps) on every sample interval (default 15s). Hundreds of process spawns over a long run for a single int.
